ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Se'em woo en nɔŋɛ a sɔ bii a ma gana mam ka nari ma. Se'em woo me n nɔŋɛ a dayua bii poyua gana mam ka nari mam.
translated: I don't want to go to the farm because I'm afraid of snakes. I also don't want to go to the river because I'm afraid of crocodiles.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Ti ba yakɛ Ateko la yakenɛ deyima la ha, Gerasene.
translated: They arrived at the region of the Gerasenes.

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: Gee va'am bihereba la n nyɛ a dayua wa la, ba ɛbegɛ la taaba yeti, ‘Asooevaaɛ daana la n ŋwana. Wa'am ya na, ti tu ku en gee sɔi vaaɛ.’
translated: When the owner of the house comes and sees that his house has been broken into, they will say to each other, ‘The owner of the house is coming. Come, let us run away before he catches us.’

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Gee mam yeti ya yeti, da lerege se'em en tuue fum. Gee se'em han lobe fum zuɔ sapɛka, ŋmɛregɛ gɔbega sapɛka la pa'ahɛ en.
translated: If you have ears, listen to what I am telling you. If you hear something bad, don't keep it to yourself.

ref: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
text: Malema kiinduma, la Farasiiduma la n wum makerɛ wa la, ba baŋɛ ti a yeti la bamam.
translated: Malema is a liar, and Farasiiduma who heard what he said, they know that he is lying.
